title: EPA Releases Proposal to Rescind Obama-Era Endangerment Finding, Regulations that Paved the Way for Electric Vehicle Mandates
content: EPA Administrator Lee Zeldin, joined by U.S. Secretary of Energy Chris Wright, Indiana Governor Mike Braun, and other officials, announced in Indiana a proposal to rescind the 2009 Endangerment Finding, which has underpinned over $1 trillion in vehicle greenhouse gas regulations including the Biden-Harris EV mandate; if finalized, the rule would repeal all GHG standards for light-, medium-, and heavy-duty vehicles and engines, citing updated scientific data and aiming to restore consumer choice, reduce costs, and save Americans an estimated $54 billion annually, with a public comment period to follow.
author: Analysts Account
